title: [EPIC] JV - Japanese-to-Vietnamese local MT replacement, benchmark, release, and auto-update

labels.name: priority:P0
labels.description: Must-have for the first usable product increment

labels.name: level: epic
labels.description: Tracking work package; not the smallest assignable unit

body:
## Summary

Coordinate research, benchmark, implementation, default flip, release, and auto-update work required for local Japanese-to-Vietnamese MT to replace Google Translation safely.

## Why is this needed?

The product goal is realtime Japanese meeting audio to Vietnamese terminal subtitles without mandatory cloud translation. Google remains the default until local MT passes measured quality, latency, privacy, and release-readiness gates.

## Current context

This WBS comes from tentacle research on 2026-05-21. Current repo state: mt_provider still defaults to google; src/bin/mt_bench.rs emits a pending lf-04-v1 fixture; local OPUS-MT provider exists behind local-mt but lacks benchmark-proven realtime replacement evidence; config has explicit mt_cloud_fallback consent; #372 closed with routing/fixture only; #383 tracks 60fps render performance; PR #407 aligns docs with local-first STT but does not make local MT default.

## Inputs

- Research handoffs: ja-vi-mt-science, ja-vi-mt-benchmark, ja-vi-mt-runtime, ja-vi-mt-release-update, ja-vi-mt-wbs.
- Existing code: src/providers/mod.rs, src/providers/google/mt.rs, src/providers/local/mt.rs, src/providers/mt/routing.rs, src/bin/mt_bench.rs, src/config/mod.rs, src/main.rs, .github/workflows/release.yml.
- Existing issues: #368 local-first epic, #372 closed LF-04 routing/fixture, #383 60fps gate, PR #407 docs alignment.

## Outputs

- One benchmark-approved local MT default path, or an explicit decision to keep Google as default with documented gaps.
- Project #2 issue set for model research, benchmark, provider runtime, config, UI/perf, docs, release, updater, and verification.
- Release roadmap from model discovery through GA with rollback and auto-update.

## Implementation approach

- Execute child issues in WBS waves: research in parallel, benchmark harness, candidate implementations, head-to-head decision, runtime provider, default flip, UI/perf/docs/security/soak gates, release packaging, auto-update, GA.
- Do not flip default until benchmark artifact passes all gates and nfr/security reviews are clean.
- Treat code-signing certificate, dataset license, and model-in-installer decisions as explicit blockers rather than assumptions.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] All child JV issues are closed or explicitly deferred with rationale.
- [ ] docs/evidence contains benchmark, performance, security, soak, and release-smoke evidence for the chosen path.
- [ ] Fresh install with no Google fallback consent can produce ja->vi subtitles locally when the model is installed or bundled.
- [ ] Rollback to Google remains explicit and user-controlled.
- [ ] Project #2 contains this epic and all child issues.

## Test cases / evidence gates

- [ ] Goal evaluation checks child issue closure, benchmark verdict, release artifact validation, and updater smoke evidence.
- [ ] nfr-verification-gate, tui-security-auditor, tui-rust-code-reviewer, and tui-soak-monitor all produce CLEAN or documented BLOCKED verdicts before GA.

## Dependencies

- Extends #368.
- Supersedes closed #372 with live benchmark and replacement work.
- Depends on #383 or an equivalent 60fps performance gate before GA.
- Aligns with PR #407 docs but requires new docs after local MT default is proven.

## Agent routing

Orchestration: tentacle-orchestration. Research: opus-class research. Build: claude-sonnet-4.6+ implementation agents. Review: tui-rust-code-reviewer, tui-security-auditor, nfr-verification-gate, tui-soak-monitor.

## Project / WBS

- WBS key: JV-EPIC
- Wave: Epic
- Project target: magicpro97/project/2
- Labels: type: epic, level: epic, area: providers, area: verification, provider: local, priority:P0

## Duplicate / extension note

New epic. It does not reopen #372; it tracks the post-fixture work needed to make local MT benchmark-proven and release-ready.
